Skip to contents
astrocoords 0.0.1
  • Get started
  • Reference
    • Working with time

astrocoords

astrocoords is an R package for basic astronomical coordinate and time conversion.

Core coordinate and time calculations in astrocoords are based on the ERFA library.

It currently supports:

  • ICRS and Galactic sky coordinates
  • angular separation on the sphere
  • coordinate matching between catalogs (coord_match(), coord_nearest()), including the kdtree method
  • spatial join helpers for data.frames (coord_left_join(), coord_nearest_join())
  • simple frame transformation between ICRS and Galactic
  • Julian Date / Modified Julian Date conversion

Installation

You can install the development version of astrocoords from GitHub with:

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("uskovgs/astrocoords")

Quick examples

Create coordinates in the ICRS frame:

library(astrocoords)
#> 
#> Attaching package: 'astrocoords'
#> The following object is masked from 'package:graphics':
#> 
#>     frame

x <- ra_dec(c(10, 20), c(30, 40))
x
#> <sky_coord[2] icrs>
#> [1] 00h40m00.0s +30°00'00" 01h19m60.0s +40°00'00"

Transform them to Galactic coordinates:

transform_to(x, galactic())
#> <sky_coord[2] galactic>
#> [1] 119°59'08.0" -32°48'23" 128°50'58.6" -22°32'36"

Measure angular separation in arcseconds:

separation(ra_dec(10, 20), ra_dec(11, 21))
#> [1] 4932.552

Find nearest matches between two coordinate vectors:

coord_nearest(
  ra_dec(c(10, 20), c(0, 0)),
  ra_dec(c(10.001, 19.999), c(0, 0)),
  unit = "arcsec",
  method = "kdtree"
)
#>   x_id y_id sep
#> 1    1    1 3.6
#> 2    2    2 3.6

Parse a compact coordinate string:

parse_coord("J230631.0+155633")
#> <sky_coord[1] icrs>
#> [1] 23h06m31.0s +15°56'33"

Work with Julian Date:

jd <- datetime_to_jd(as.POSIXct("2026-03-08 12:34:56", tz = "UTC"))
jd
#> [1] 2461108

jd_to_datetime(jd, tz = "UTC")
#> [1] "2026-03-08 12:34:56 UTC"
